name: Sican Mask

distinctive_features: avian features such as beaks and wings
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: symmetrical facial features with a central nose or beak projection

domain_specific_visual.iconography: depicts the Sican Deity with avian features
domain_specific_visual.material: crafted from gold and other metals

domain_specific_functional.cultural_role: used in rituals and as a symbol of authority

relationships.description: The mask is a representation of the Sican Deity in religious ceremonies.
The avian features on the mask are related to Naylamp, a key figure in Sican mythology.

contextual_attributes.origin: Lambayeque region, north coast of Peru
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents religious and political power in Sican culture

contrastive_constraints: NOT a mask with human facial features
NOT associated with Inca or Moche cultures
